message:
feat(scripts): add E2E/conformance/bench runner scripts (beads_rust-no03)

Add comprehensive runner scripts for test suites:
- scripts/e2e.sh: Quick E2E tests (6 core tests)
- scripts/e2e_full.sh: Full E2E suite with parallel option
- scripts/conformance.sh: br vs bd comparison tests
- scripts/bench.sh: Benchmarks with criterion and bd comparison

All scripts support:
- JSON output (--json) for CI integration
- Environment guards for long-running tests
- Artifact logging to target/test-artifacts/
- Dataset selection flags
- Timeout configuration

Also closes beads_rust-b4nj (integrity guard) and beads_rust-trwc (unit tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"beads_rust-gs0","title":"Phase 5: Polish & Conformance - Production Readiness","description":"# Phase 5: Polish & Conformance

## Goals
Deliver a production-ready `br` with **classic bd parity**, comprehensive testing, and ergonomic utility commands that do not expand scope beyond the three planning docs.

## Deliverables (children)
### Utility/Inspection Commands
- `doctor` (read-only diagnostics; no auto-fixes)
- `info`, `where`, `version`
- `stats/status`, `count`, `stale`, `orphans`
- `defer` / `undefer`
- `epic status` + `close-eligible`
- `graph` (deps visualization)
- `audit` (interactions.jsonl)
- `history` (.br_history) + `changelog`
- `q` (quick capture)
- `lint` (template/config validation)
- `saved queries` (save/run/list/delete)
- CSV export for list/export

### Test & QA Infrastructure
- Unit test infra and specs
- Snapshot/golden tests for human output
- E2E integration tests (CLI workflows)
- Conformance harness (bd vs br JSON parity)
- CI pipeline (fmt + clippy + tests)

### UX & Performance Polish
- Color output (opt-in or auto)
- Progress indicators for long ops
- Shell completions
- Performance benchmarks (startup + common paths)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conformance suite green; JSON parity with bd for classic commands.
- Utility commands are stable, documented, and tested.
- CI runs `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, and all tests.
- Performance baselines recorded; regressions detectable.

## Notes
- No daemon, no git hooks, no auto-git behavior.
- Utility commands must be read-only unless explicitly documented.

 {"id":"beads_rust-gu7b","title":"Code coverage reporting with tarpaulin","description":"# Code Coverage Reporting

## Overview
Configure cargo-tarpaulin for coverage reporting and CI integration.

## Installation
```bash
cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
```

## Configuration

### tarpaulin.toml
```toml
[coverage]
ignore-tests = true
follow-exec = true
timeout = "300s"
out = ["Html", "Lcov", "Json"]
output-dir = "coverage"

[coverage.run]
jobs = 4

[coverage.exclude]
# Exclude benchmarks and examples
paths = ["benches/*", "examples/*"]

# Exclude test helpers
test-patterns = ["test_helpers::*"]
```

## Coverage Targets

### Module Coverage Goals
| Module | Target | Priority |
|--------|--------|----------|
| src/storage/ | 90%+ | Critical - core data layer |
| src/sync/ | 85%+ | Critical - data integrity |
| src/cli/commands/ | 80%+ | High - user-facing |
| src/model/ | 85%+ | High - domain logic |
| src/format/ | 75%+ | Medium - output formatting |
| src/util/ | 70%+ | Medium - helpers |

### Overall Goals
- Minimum threshold: 70%
- Target: 85%+
- Stretch: 90%+

## Logging Requirements

### Coverage Run Logging
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# coverage.sh - Run with logging

echo "coverage_run_start: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo "coverage_config: ignore_tests=true output_dir=coverage"

# Run tarpaulin with verbose output
cargo tarpaulin --out Html --out Lcov --out Json 2>&1 | tee coverage/tarpaulin.log

# Parse and log results
COVERAGE=$(jq '.coverage_percent' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)
LINES_COVERED=$(jq '.covered_lines' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)
TOTAL_LINES=$(jq '.total_lines' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)

echo "coverage_result: percent=${COVERAGE} covered=${LINES_COVERED} total=${TOTAL_LINES}"

# Per-module breakdown
echo "coverage_module_breakdown:"
jq -r '.files[] | "  \(.path): \(.coverage)%"' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json

echo "coverage_run_end: $(date -Iseconds)"
```

### CI Integration Logging
```yaml
# .github/workflows/coverage.yml
- name: Generate coverage
  run: |
    echo "::group::Coverage Generation"
    cargo tarpaulin --out Lcov --out Json 2>&1 | tee coverage.log
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    # Log summary
    COVERAGE=$(jq '.coverage_percent' tarpaulin-report.json)
    echo "coverage_summary: ${COVERAGE}%"
    echo "::set-output name=coverage::${COVERAGE}"
    
    # Fail if below threshold
    if (( $(echo "$COVERAGE < 70" | bc -l) )); then
      echo "::error::Coverage ${COVERAGE}% below minimum 70%"
      exit 1
    fi

- name: Upload to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
  with:
    files: lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
    verbose: true
```

### Per-Module Coverage Logging
```rust
// In test harness, log coverage per module
#[cfg(test)]
mod coverage_logging {
    use std::process::Command;
    
    fn log_module_coverage(module: &str) {
        let output = Command::new("cargo")
            .args(["tarpaulin", "--out", "Json", "--packages", module])
            .output()
            .expect("tarpaulin failed");
        
        if output.status.success() {
            let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
            let coverage = json["coverage_percent"].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
            println!("coverage_module: {}={:.1}%", module, coverage);
        }
    }
}
```

## Usage
```bash
# Generate coverage report
cargo tarpaulin --out Html

# View report
open coverage/tarpaulin-report.html

# Quick coverage check
cargo tarpaulin --out Stdout --skip-clean

# Module-specific coverage
cargo tarpaulin --packages beads_rust --out Html -- --test storage
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tarpaulin.toml configured with proper exclusions
- [ ] Coverage report generates locally
- [ ] CI workflow for coverage with threshold check
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Per-module breakdown logging
- [ ] Minimum 70% coverage enforced
- [ ] HTML + Lcov + JSON outputs generated

 {"id":"beads_rust-h2c","title":"Audit events: insertion rules + retrieval ordering","description":"# Audit Events (events table)

## Purpose
Implement the audit/event model exactly as classic bd: every mutation writes an event row inside the same transaction, and events are **local DB only** (never exported to JSONL). This bead defines event schema, insertion rules, and retrieval ordering.

## Schema (SQLite)
- `events` table: `id` (autoinc), `issue_id`, `event_type`, `actor`, `old_value`, `new_value`, `comment`, `created_at`.
- Indexes: `events.issue_id`, `events.event_type`, `events.created_at`, `events.actor` (see schema bead).

## Event Types (classic)
- `created`
- `updated`
- `status_changed`
- `closed`
- `reopened`
- `commented`
- `dependency_added` / `dependency_removed`
- `label_added` / `label_removed`
- `deleted` (tombstone) / `restored` (if restore supported)
- `compacted` (schema includes compaction fields even if compaction excluded)

## Insertion Rules (must be atomic with mutation)
- **CreateIssue**: emit `created` with actor.
- **UpdateIssue**: emit:
  - `status_changed` for non-terminal status transitions.
  - `closed` when status becomes `closed`.
  - `reopened` when moving from `closed` to `open`.
  - `updated` for other field changes.
- **CloseIssue**: `closed` with `comment` = close reason.
- **ReopenIssue**: `reopened` (and optionally a comment if `--reason` adds a comment).
- **DeleteIssue**: `deleted` with delete reason.
- **RestoreIssue** (if supported): `restored`.
- **Dep add/remove**: `dependency_added` / `dependency_removed` with comment like:
  - `Added dependency: <issue> <type> <depends_on>`
  - `Removed dependency on <depends_on>`
- **Label add/remove**: `label_added` / `label_removed`.
- **Comment add**: `commented` with `comment` text.

## Retrieval Semantics
- `GetEvents(issue_id, limit)` returns **newest first** (created_at DESC).
- Events are **not** exported to JSONL.
- CLI should only expose events via `show --events` (if implemented) or similar.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every mutation path inserts exactly one appropriate event row.
- Events are created **within the same transaction** as the mutation + dirty mark.
- `GetEvents` ordering is `created_at DESC`.
- JSONL export/import ignores events.

## Tests
- Unit tests for each mutation path verifying event_type + actor + timestamps.
- Ordering test for `GetEvents` (DESC).
- Integration test: create/update/close/dep/label/comment produce expected event sequence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:05:22.134434351Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:47:59.406508805Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:47:59.406508805Z","close_reason":"Implemented audit events module: schema, insertion functions, GetEvents with DESC ordering, 13 unit tests passing"}

 {"id":"beads_rust-i7s","title":"where Command Implementation","description":"# where Command Implementation

## Purpose
Report the active `.beads` directory location, including redirects and prefix detection.

## Behavior
- Find active beads dir (respect `BEADS_DIR` if set).
- If `.beads/redirect` exists, follow it and report `redirected_from`.
- Detect prefix:
  - Prefer DB config `issue_prefix` if DB available.
  - Else parse prefix from first JSONL line ID.
- Error when no beads dir:
  - Text: error + hint, exit 1.
  - JSON: `{ "error": "no beads directory found" }`, exit 1.

## JSON Output
```json
{
  "path": "/abs/path/.beads",
  "redirected_from": "/abs/other/.beads",
  "prefix": "bd",
  "database_path": "/abs/path/.beads/beads.db"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correct path resolution with and without redirect.
- Prefix detection matches classic order.
- Error behavior matches bd.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyOwl","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:32.897767057Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T06:00:03.997180494Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T06:00:03.997180494Z","close_reason":"Implemented where command with text and JSON output modes.  {"id":"beads_rust-j57","title":"audit Command (interactions.jsonl)","description":"# audit Command (interactions.jsonl)

## Purpose
Optional agent interaction logging to `.beads/interactions.jsonl`, matching bd audit schemas. Read/write only; no daemon required.

## CLI
```
br audit record [--kind ... --issue-id ... --model ... --prompt ... --response ... --tool-name ... --exit-code ... --error ... --stdin]
br audit label <entry-id> --label <label> [--reason <text>]
```

## Storage
- Append-only JSONL at `.beads/interactions.jsonl`.

 {"id":"beads_rust-ja0","title":"blocked Command Implementation","description":"# blocked Command

## Purpose
List blocked issues using `blocked_issues_cache` and show blockers. This command provides visibility into issues that cannot be worked on until their dependencies are resolved.

## CLI
```
br blocked [--limit N] [--verbose] [--type <type>] [--priority <priority>] [--label <label>]
```

## Flags
- `--limit N`: Maximum number of blocked issues to return (default: 50, 0 = unlimited).
- `--verbose`: Include full blocker details in text output.
- `--type <type>`: Filter by issue type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore).
- `--priority <priority>`: Filter by priority (0-4 or P0-P4).
- `--label <label>`: Filter by label (can be repeated, uses AND logic).
- `--json`: Output as JSON.
- `--robot`: Machine-readable output.

## Behavior
1. Read blocked issues from `blocked_issues_cache` table (not recalculated on the fly).
2. For each blocked issue, retrieve immediate blockers (issues with `blocks` dependency).
3. Apply filters (type, priority, labels).
4. Sort by priority (ascending), then by number of blockers (descending).
5. Return up to `--limit` issues.

## Data Model
```rust
pub struct BlockedIssue {
    pub issue: Issue,
    pub blocked_by_count: usize,
    pub blocked_by: Vec<String>,  // IDs of immediate blockers
}
```

## Output

### JSON
```json
[
  {
    "id": "bd-abc12",
    "title": "Implement feature",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 1,
    "blocked_by_count": 2,
    "blocked_by": ["bd-xyz89", "bd-def34"]
  }
]
```

### Text Output (default)
```
Blocked Issues (3 total):

1. [bd-abc12] P1 Implement feature
   Blocked by: bd-xyz89, bd-def34 (2 issues)

2. [bd-ghi56] P2 Add tests
   Blocked by: bd-abc12 (1 issue)
```

### Text Output (--verbose)
```
Blocked Issues (3 total):

1. [bd-abc12] P1 Implement feature
   Blocked by:
     • bd-xyz89: Database schema [P0] [in_progress]
     • bd-def34: API design [P1] [open]

2. [bd-ghi56] P2 Add tests
   Blocked by:
     • bd-abc12: Implement feature [P1] [blocked]
```

## Error Handling
- **DatabaseNotInitialized**: If beads not initialized → suggest `br init`.
- **CacheInvalid**: If cache is stale, trigger rebuild transparently.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!("Fetching blocked issues from cache");
tracing::debug!(count = blocked.len(), "Found {} blocked issues", count);
for issue in &blocked {
    tracing::trace!(
        id = %issue.id,
        blockers = ?issue.blocked_by,
        "Blocked issue: {} blocked by {:?}",
        issue.id,
        issue.blocked_by
    );
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reads from cache; does not recompute on every call.
- JSON shape matches bd.
- Filters work correctly (type, priority, labels).
- Verbose mode shows blocker details.

 {"id":"beads_rust-u23","title":"reopen Command Implementation","description":"# reopen Command

## Purpose
Explicitly reopen closed issues (distinct from update --status open), matching bd behavior with optional comment insertion.

## CLI
```
br reopen <id...> [--reason <text>]
```

## Flags
- `<id...>`: One or more issue IDs (partial resolution). If none, uses last-touched.
- `--reason <text>`: Reason for reopening, stored as a comment on the issue.

## Behavior
1. Resolve issue ID(s) via partial matching.
2. For each issue:
   - Validate issue is currently closed; warn if already open.
   - Set `status=open`.
   - Clear `closed_at` field (set to null).
   - If `--reason` provided, add a comment: "Reopened: <reason>".
   - Emit `reopened` event to event log.
   - Mark issue as dirty for export.
3. Rebuild blocked cache (reopened issues may become blockers).

## Output

### JSON
Array of reopened Issue objects:
```json
[
  {
    "id": "bd-abc12",
    "title": "Implement feature",
    "status": "open",
    "closed_at": null
  }
]
```

### Text Output
```
✓ Reopened bd-abc12: Implement feature
  Reason: Found additional edge case
```

## Error Handling
- **IssueNotFound**: If ID doesnt resolve → error with suggestions.
- **AmbiguousId**: If ID resolves to multiple → error with candidate list.
- **AlreadyOpen**: Warning (not error) if issue already open.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!(id = %issue.id, "Reopening issue");
tracing::debug!(previous_status = ?old_status, "Issue was previously {:?}", old_status);
tracing::info!(id = %issue.id, reason = ?reason, "Issue reopened");
if let Some(reason) = reason {
    tracing::debug!(id = %issue.id, "Adding reopen comment");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Comment inserted when reason provided.
- closed_at is cleared (set to null).
- JSON shape matches bd.
- Multiple IDs work correctly.

## Purpose
Identify open/in-progress issues referenced in git commits but not yet closed. This is a read-only view used to catch missing close operations.

- Prefix is read from DB config `issue_prefix` (fallback default `bd`).
- Supports hierarchical IDs (e.g., `bd-abc.1`).
- For each issue ID, keep only the **latest (most recent)** commit reference.
- Only return issues whose status is `open` or `in_progress`.
- If not a git repo, missing `.beads/`, or missing DB: return empty list (no error).

br orphans [--details] [--fix]

- `--details`: include latest commit hash + message in human output.
- `--fix`: **interactive** close flow (no JSON batch). Should prompt for confirmation

  }
]
```

 {"id":"beads_rust-uin","title":"prime Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br prime` command for displaying context about the current beads state. This is designed for AI agents to quickly understand the project's issue state after context loss (e.g., after compaction or starting a new session).

## CLI Interface
```
br prime [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --full                      Include all context (ready + in_progress + recent)
  --ready-only                Only show ready issues
  --limit <N>                 Limit issues per category (default: 10)
  --json                      Output as JSON
  --robot                     Machine-readable output
```

## Output Structure

The prime command outputs a comprehensive snapshot for agent context recovery:

```

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## Project: beads_rust
Prefix: beads_rust-
Schema: v3
Issues: 156 total (42 open, 8 in_progress, 106 closed)

[P0] beads_rust-abc123  Fix critical auth bug
     Assignee: alice | Updated: 2 hours ago
     Blocked by: (none - ready to work)

[P1] beads_rust-def456  Implement user dashboard
     Assignee: bob | Updated: 1 day ago
     Blocked by: beads_rust-xyz789 (Config system)

## Ready to Work (top 10)
Unblocked issues by priority:

[P0] beads_rust-ghi789  Security patch for XSS
[P1] beads_rust-jkl012  Add pagination to list API
[P1] beads_rust-mno345  Refactor error handling
...

## Recently Updated (top 5)
Latest activity:

[closed] beads_rust-pqr678  Setup CI/CD pipeline
         Closed 3 hours ago by alice

[updated] beads_rust-stu901  Database migration scripts
          Updated 5 hours ago (status: open → in_progress)

## Critical Blockers
Issues blocking the most work:

1. beads_rust-xyz789 (Config system) - blocks 5 issues
2. beads_rust-vwx234 (Auth middleware) - blocks 3 issues

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Commands: br ready | br show <id> | br update <id> --status=in_progress
```

### Implementation
```rust
fn prime(&self, opts: PrimeOptions) -> Result<PrimeContext> {
    let stats = self.get_stats()?;
    let in_progress = self.list_issues(ListQuery { 
        status: Some(Status::InProgress),
        limit: opts.limit,
        ..Default::default()
    })?;
    let ready = self.get_ready_issues(ReadyFilters {
        limit: opts.limit,
        ..Default::default()
    })?;
    let recent = self.get_recent_activity(opts.limit)?;
    let blockers = self.find_critical_blockers(5)?;
    
    Ok(PrimeContext {
        stats,
        in_progress,
        ready,
        recent,
        blockers,
    })
}
```

## JSON Output
```json
{
  "project": "beads_rust",
  "stats": {
    "total": 156,
    "open": 42,
    "in_progress": 8,
    "closed": 106
  },
  "in_progress": [...],
  "ready": [...],
  "recent": [...],
  "critical_blockers": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Show project summary (name, prefix, counts)
- [ ] List in-progress issues
- [ ] List ready-to-work issues
- [ ] Show recent activity
- [ ] Identify critical blockers
- [ ] Human-readable and JSON output
- [ ] Configurable limits
- [ ] Fast execution (< 100ms)

## Dependencies
- Requires stats Command infrastructure
- Requires ready Command infrastructure
- Requires blocked Command infrastructure

## Rationale
AI agents lose context frequently due to context window limits. The prime command provides everything an agent needs to resume work effectively. It answers: "What was I working on? What can I work on? What's blocking progress?"

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Benchmark runner - measures br performance with timing and optional comparisons.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   scripts/bench.sh                    # Run all benchmarks
+#   scripts/bench.sh --quick            # Run quick comparison only (no criterion)
+#   scripts/bench.sh --criterion        # Run criterion benchmarks only
+#   scripts/bench.sh --compare          # Compare br vs bd performance
+#   scripts/bench.sh --json             # Output summary as JSON
+#   scripts/bench.sh --save NAME        # Save baseline as NAME
+#   scripts/bench.sh --baseline NAME    # Compare against baseline NAME
+#
+# Environment:
+#   HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1       Enable artifact logging to target/test-artifacts/
+#   BR_BINARY=/path/to/br     Override br binary location
+#   BD_BINARY=/path/to/bd     Override bd binary location (for comparison)
+#   BENCH_TIMEOUT=600         Per-benchmark timeout in seconds (default: 300)
+#   BENCH_DATASET=beads_rust  Dataset to use for benchmarks
+#
+# Output:
+#   - Exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure
+#   - Summary JSON written to target/test-artifacts/benchmark_summary.json
+#   - Criterion reports in target/criterion/
+#   - Benchmark artifacts in target/test-artifacts/benchmark/
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
+ARTIFACTS_DIR="${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/test-artifacts"
+CRITERION_DIR="${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/criterion"
+
+# Configuration
+JSON_OUTPUT=0
+QUICK_ONLY=0
+CRITERION_ONLY=0
+COMPARE_BD=0
+SAVE_BASELINE=""
+USE_BASELINE=""
+TIMEOUT="${BENCH_TIMEOUT:-300}"
+DATASET="${BENCH_DATASET:-beads_rust}"
+
+log() {
+    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        echo -e "\033[36m[bench]\033[0m $*"
+    fi
+}
+
+error() {
+    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 0 ]]; then

+    fi
+

+    if timeout "$TIMEOUT" cargo bench $baseline_args 2>&1 | tee "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/benchmark/criterion.log"; then
+        RESULTS+=("{\"benchmark\":\"criterion\",\"result\":\"pass\"}")
+        log "  Criterion benchmarks: PASS"
+    else
+        RESULTS+=("{\"benchmark\":\"criterion\",\"result\":\"fail\"}")
+        error "  Criterion benchmarks: FAIL"
+    fi

+    if [[ -d "$CRITERION_DIR" ]]; then
+        log "  Criterion reports: $CRITERION_DIR"
+    fi

+run_bd_comparison() {
+    log "Running br vs bd performance comparison..."
+
+    # Find bd binary
+    BD_PATH="${BD_BINARY:-}"
+    if [[ -z "$BD_PATH" ]]; then
+        for candidate in "/data/projects/beads/.bin/beads" "$HOME/go/bin/bd" "$(command -v bd 2>/dev/null || true)"; do

+        local br_end=$(date +%s.%N)

+        local bd_start=$(date +%s.%N)
+        "$BD_PATH" $op >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+        local bd_end=$(date +%s.%N)
+        local bd_time=$(echo "$bd_end - $bd_start" | bc)
+
+        local speedup=$(echo "scale=2; $bd_time / $br_time" | bc 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A")
+
+        echo "    {\"op\": \"$op\", \"br_ms\": $br_time, \"bd_ms\": $bd_time, \"speedup\": $speedup}" >> "$comparison_file"
+        log "  $op: br=${br_time}s bd=${bd_time}s speedup=${speedup}x"
+    done
+
+    echo "  ]" >> "$comparison_file"
+    echo "}" >> "$comparison_file"
+
+    RESULTS+=("{\"benchmark\":\"bd_comparison\",\"result\":\"pass\",\"report\":\"$comparison_file\"}")
+    log "  Comparison report: $comparison_file"

+# Run benchmarks based on flags
+if [[ "$QUICK_ONLY" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    run_quick_benchmark

+else
+    run_quick_benchmark

+    if [[ "$COMPARE_BD" -eq 1 ]]; then

+    fi

+{

+

+

+log "Duration: ${TOTAL_DURATION}s"
+log "Criterion: $CRITERION_DIR"
+log "Artifacts: $ARTIFACTS_DIR/benchmark"
+log "============================================"
+
+exit 0

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Conformance test runner - compares br and bd outputs for parity.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   scripts/conformance.sh                  # Run conformance tests
+#   scripts/conformance.sh --json           # Output summary as JSON
+#   scripts/conformance.sh --verbose        # Show test output
+#   scripts/conformance.sh --filter PATTERN # Run only tests matching PATTERN
+#   scripts/conformance.sh --check-bd       # Verify bd is available first
+#
+# Environment:
+#   HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1       Enable artifact logging to target/test-artifacts/
+#   HARNESS_PRESERVE_SUCCESS=1  Keep artifacts even on success
+#   BR_BINARY=/path/to/br     Override br binary location
+#   BD_BINARY=/path/to/bd     Override bd binary location (required)
+#   CONFORMANCE_TIMEOUT=180   Per-test timeout in seconds (default: 120)
+#   CONFORMANCE_STRICT=1      Fail on any normalization differences
+#
+# Requirements:
+#   - Both br (Rust) and bd (Go) binaries must be available
+#   - bd is typically found at /data/projects/beads/.bin/beads
+#
+# Output:
+#   - Exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure, 2 on bd unavailable
+#   - Summary JSON written to target/test-artifacts/conformance_summary.json
+#   - Artifacts in target/test-artifacts/conformance/<test>/
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
+ARTIFACTS_DIR="${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/test-artifacts"
+
+# Conformance tests
+CONFORMANCE_TESTS=(
+    conformance
+    conformance_edge_cases
+    conformance_labels_comments
+)
+
+# Configuration
+JSON_OUTPUT=0
+VERBOSE=0
+FILTER=""
+CHECK_BD=0
+TIMEOUT="${CONFORMANCE_TIMEOUT:-120}"
+
+# Binary paths
+BR_BIN="${BR_BINARY:-}"
+BD_BIN="${BD_BINARY:-}"
+
+log() {
+    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        echo -e "\033[35m[conformance]\033[0m $*"
+    fi
+}
+
+error() {
+    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        echo -e "\033[31m[conformance] ERROR:\033[0m $*" >&2
+    fi
+}
+
+warn() {
+    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        echo -e "\033[33m[conformance] WARN:\033[0m $*" >&2
+    fi
+}
+
+usage() {
+    head -n 25 "$0" | tail -n +2 | sed 's/^# //' | sed 's/^#//'
+    exit 0
+}
+
+# Find bd binary
+find_bd() {
+    if [[ -n "$BD_BIN" ]] && [[ -x "$BD_BIN" ]]; then
+        echo "$BD_BIN"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    # Common locations for bd
+    local candidates=(
+        "/data/projects/beads/.bin/beads"
+        "$HOME/go/bin/bd"
+        "$HOME/.local/bin/bd"
+        "$(command -v bd 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    )
+
+    for candidate in "${candidates[@]}"; do
+        if [[ -n "$candidate" ]] && [[ -x "$candidate" ]]; then
+            echo "$candidate"
+            return 0
+        fi
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+# Parse arguments
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --json)
+            JSON_OUTPUT=1
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --verbose|-v)
+            VERBOSE=1
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --filter)
+            FILTER="$2"
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        --check-bd)
+            CHECK_BD=1
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --help|-h)
+            usage
+            ;;
+        *)
+            error "Unknown argument: $1"
+            usage
+            ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+# Check for bd availability
+log "Checking for bd (Go beads) binary..."
+if ! BD_PATH=$(find_bd); then
+    error "bd binary not found!"
+    error "Conformance tests require both br (Rust) and bd (Go) binaries."
+    error "Set BD_BINARY=/path/to/bd or install bd."
+    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 1 ]]; then
+        echo '{"error": "bd_not_found", "message": "bd binary required for conformance tests"}'
+    fi
+    exit 2
+fi
+
+log "Found bd at: $BD_PATH"
+BD_VERSION=$("$BD_PATH" version 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "unknown")
+log "bd version: $BD_VERSION"
+
+if [[ "$CHECK_BD" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    log "bd is available. Exiting (--check-bd mode)."
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+# Ensure br build is up to date
+log "Building br binary..."
+cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
+cargo build --release --quiet 2>/dev/null || cargo build --release
+
+BR_PATH="${BR_BINARY:-$(cargo metadata --format-version=1 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.target_directory')/release/br}"
+if [[ ! -x "$BR_PATH" ]]; then
+    BR_PATH="$PROJECT_ROOT/target/release/br"
+fi
+log "Using br at: $BR_PATH"
+BR_VERSION=$("$BR_PATH" version 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "unknown")
+log "br version: $BR_VERSION"
+
+# Export binary paths for tests
+export BR_BINARY="$BR_PATH"
+export BD_BINARY="$BD_PATH"
+
+# Create artifacts directory
+mkdir -p "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/conformance"
+
+# Run tests
+PASSED=0
+FAILED=0
+SKIPPED=0
+START_TIME=$(date +%s)
+RESULTS=()
+
+log "Running conformance tests (${#CONFORMANCE_TESTS[@]} test files)..."
+log "Artifacts: $ARTIFACTS_DIR/conformance"
+
+for test in "${CONFORMANCE_TESTS[@]}"; do
+    # Apply filter if specified
+    if [[ -n "$FILTER" ]] && [[ ! "$test" =~ $FILTER ]]; then
+        ((SKIPPED++))
+        continue
+    fi
+
+    log "  Running: $test"
+
+    TEST_START=$(date +%s.%N)
+
+    NOCAPTURE_FLAG=""
+    if [[ "$VERBOSE" -eq 1 ]]; then
+        NOCAPTURE_FLAG="--nocapture"
+    fi
+
+    if timeout "$TIMEOUT" cargo test --test "$test" -- $NOCAPTURE_FLAG 2>&1; then
+        RESULT="pass"
+        ((PASSED++))
+    else
+        RESULT="fail"
+        ((FAILED++))
+    fi
+
+    TEST_END=$(date +%s.%N)
+    TEST_DURATION=$(echo "$TEST_END - $TEST_START" | bc 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+
+    RESULTS+=("{\"test\":\"$test\",\"result\":\"$RESULT\",\"duration_s\":$TEST_DURATION}")
+
+    if [[ "$RESULT" == "pass" ]]; then
+        log "    PASS (${TEST_DURATION}s)"
+    else
+        error "    FAIL (${TEST_DURATION}s)"
+    fi
+done
+
+END_TIME=$(date +%s)
+TOTAL_DURATION=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))
+
+# Generate summary
+SUMMARY_FILE="$ARTIFACTS_DIR/conformance_summary.json"
+RESULTS_JSON=$(printf '%s\n' "${RESULTS[@]}" | paste -sd, -)
+
+cat > "$SUMMARY_FILE" << EOF
+{
+  "suite": "conformance",
+  "generated_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
+  "passed": $PASSED,
+  "failed": $FAILED,
+  "skipped": $SKIPPED,
+  "total": $((PASSED + FAILED)),
+  "duration_s": $TOTAL_DURATION,
+  "binaries": {
+    "br": "$BR_PATH",
+    "br_version": "$BR_VERSION",
+    "bd": "$BD_PATH",
+    "bd_version": "$BD_VERSION"
+  },
+  "artifacts_dir": "$ARTIFACTS_DIR/conformance",
+  "results": [$RESULTS_JSON]
+}
+EOF
+
+log "Summary written to: $SUMMARY_FILE"
+
+if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    cat "$SUMMARY_FILE"
+fi
+
+# Final summary
+log "============================================"
+log "Conformance Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
+log "Duration: ${TOTAL_DURATION}s"
+log "br: $BR_VERSION | bd: $BD_VERSION"
+log "Artifacts: $ARTIFACTS_DIR/conformance"
+log "============================================"
+
+if [[ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ]]; then
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+exit 0

+
+log() {
+    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        echo -e "\033[32m[e2e_full]\033[0m $*"
+    fi
+}
+
+error() {
+    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        echo -e "\033[31m[e2e_full] ERROR:\033[0m $*" >&2
+    fi
+}
+
+warn() {
+    if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        echo -e "\033[33m[e2e_full] WARN:\033[0m $*" >&2
+    fi
+}
+
+usage() {
+    head -n 25 "$0" | tail -n +2 | sed 's/^# //' | sed 's/^#//'
+    exit 0
+}
+
+# Parse arguments
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --json)
+            JSON_OUTPUT=1
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --verbose|-v)
+            VERBOSE=1
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --filter)
+            FILTER="$2"
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        --parallel)
+            PARALLEL=1
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --dataset)
+            DATASET="$2"
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        --help|-h)
+            usage
+            ;;
+        *)
+            error "Unknown argument: $1"
+            usage
+            ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+# Environment guard for long-running tests
+if [[ -z "${E2E_FULL_CONFIRM:-}" ]] && [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    warn "Full E2E suite runs all tests and may take several minutes."
+    warn "Set E2E_FULL_CONFIRM=1 or use scripts/e2e.sh for quick feedback."
+    read -p "Continue? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
+    echo
+    if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
+        log "Aborted."
+        exit 0
+    fi
+fi
+
+# Ensure build is up to date
+log "Building br binary..."
+cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
+cargo build --release --quiet 2>/dev/null || cargo build --release
+
+# Discover all E2E tests
+log "Discovering E2E tests..."
+ALL_TESTS=()
+for test_file in tests/e2e_*.rs; do
+    test_name=$(basename "$test_file" .rs)
+    # Apply filter if specified
+    if [[ -n "$FILTER" ]] && [[ ! "$test_name" =~ $FILTER ]]; then
+        continue
+    fi
+    ALL_TESTS+=("$test_name")
+done
+
+log "Found ${#ALL_TESTS[@]} E2E tests"
+
+# Create artifacts directory
+mkdir -p "$ARTIFACTS_DIR"
+
+# Export dataset if specified
+if [[ -n "$DATASET" ]]; then
+    export E2E_DATASET="$DATASET"
+    log "Using dataset: $DATASET"
+fi
+
+# Run tests
+PASSED=0
+FAILED=0
+SKIPPED=0
+START_TIME=$(date +%s)
+RESULTS=()
+
+if [[ "$PARALLEL" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    log "Running tests in parallel..."
+    CARGO_ARGS="--jobs $(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 4)"
+else
+    log "Running tests serially..."
+    CARGO_ARGS="--jobs 1"
+fi
+
+for test in "${ALL_TESTS[@]}"; do
+    log "  Running: $test"
+
+    TEST_START=$(date +%s.%N)
+
+    NOCAPTURE_FLAG=""
+    if [[ "$VERBOSE" -eq 1 ]]; then
+        NOCAPTURE_FLAG="--nocapture"
+    fi
+
+    if timeout "$TIMEOUT" cargo test --test "$test" $CARGO_ARGS -- $NOCAPTURE_FLAG >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        RESULT="pass"
+        ((PASSED++))
+    else
+        RESULT="fail"
+        ((FAILED++))
+    fi
+
+    TEST_END=$(date +%s.%N)
+    TEST_DURATION=$(echo "$TEST_END - $TEST_START" | bc 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+
+    RESULTS+=("{\"test\":\"$test\",\"result\":\"$RESULT\",\"duration_s\":$TEST_DURATION}")
+
+    if [[ "$RESULT" == "pass" ]]; then
+        log "    PASS (${TEST_DURATION}s)"
+    else
+        error "    FAIL (${TEST_DURATION}s)"
+    fi
+done
+
+END_TIME=$(date +%s)
+TOTAL_DURATION=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))
+
+# Generate summary
+SUMMARY_FILE="$ARTIFACTS_DIR/e2e_full_summary.json"
+RESULTS_JSON=$(printf '%s\n' "${RESULTS[@]}" | paste -sd, -)
+
+cat > "$SUMMARY_FILE" << EOF
+{
+  "suite": "e2e_full",
+  "generated_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
+  "passed": $PASSED,
+  "failed": $FAILED,
+  "skipped": $SKIPPED,
+  "total": $((PASSED + FAILED)),
+  "duration_s": $TOTAL_DURATION,
+  "parallel": $([[ "$PARALLEL" -eq 1 ]] && echo "true" || echo "false"),
+  "dataset": "${DATASET:-null}",
+  "artifacts_dir": "$ARTIFACTS_DIR",
+  "results": [$RESULTS_JSON]
+}
+EOF
+
+log "Summary written to: $SUMMARY_FILE"
+
+if [[ "$JSON_OUTPUT" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    cat "$SUMMARY_FILE"
+fi
+
+# Final summary
+log "============================================"
+log "Full E2E Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
+log "Duration: ${TOTAL_DURATION}s"
+log "Artifacts: $ARTIFACTS_DIR"
+log "============================================"
+
+if [[ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ]]; then
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+exit 0
